questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: The India-Russia defence partnership has transitioned from a traditional 'buyer-seller' relationship to one of 'joint development and export'. Which of the following developments best illustrates this transition?

options.text: The direct procurement of the S-400 Triumf air defence system from Russia.
The joint production of BrahMos missiles and Russia's approval of their sale to the Philippines.
The supply of 'Tushil' class frigates to the Indian Navy under a government-to-government contract.
The use of Russian technology in the development of India's Gaganyaan mission.

questions.explanation: The BrahMos project is the hallmark of joint production. The fact that Russia has approved its sale to a third country (Philippines) demonstrates the evolution from domestic use of joint technology to becoming joint exporters in the global market.
questions.takeaway: The export of BrahMos to third countries signifies a mature stage of strategic partnership where joint ventures target global markets.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Military collaboration: Joint production of Brahmos cruise missile... Russia has approved the sale of Brahmos missiles to Philippines.
